The price set on the Spokane and
Occur d'Alene reservations had not
yet been made public this forenoon,
but the Flathead Ianos were ap-
praised at from s1.25 to $3.50 per
acre, and the other reservations
will run approximately the same.


One-fifth of the fixed price of the
claim will have to be paid down.
On timber claims the balance can
be paid in annual instruments, five
years being required to acquire full
title to this class of property. Four
teen months are required for agri-
cultural lands, the balance for
these being due at the conclusion
of that period. The amended laws
require more cultivation and i'm
provement than was formerly nee
essary and the successful applicant
must comply with all these in order
to show good faith In his sworn in
tention of creating home.


A big influx Of lanl seekers is en-
pected In town tomorrow. ]. H.
Pelletier, notary public in the
Rookery building. has an engage
ment with party of 257 from Ia
coma and several hundred are COM
ing over from Seattle. Other coast
points will be represented.


The name and addresses OF win
ners In the drawing will be fur
n1Dhed the newspapers and those
mot reached In this manner will be
notified by mail. In the case of
applicants failing to avail them
selves Of the opportunity to make
choice the nearest number will
be annexed to the winning list.


The process Of registering before
. notary II extremely simple. The
applicant simply states his business
and the notary supplies the nee
ossary blank. He fills out the
spaces, swears the applicant on the
statement on the blank. sees that
4 y properly signed aud gives It to
